reason: Pinging the gateway's IP is the standard reachability test; only one option present (no distractors), so item is degenerate but the option is substantively correct.
IP addressing is hierarchical (network/host, aggregatable for routing) whereas MAC/Ethernet addressing is flat.
Receive equalizers compensate delay/amplitude distortion with a transversal (tapped delay line) filter.

unanswerable - single option, no distractors, and the lone option 'Ethernet topology' is factually wrong: FDDI is a dual counter-rotating token-passing fiber ring used for high-speed backbones/MANs, not an Ethernet topology.
Page traffic = the movement (transfer rate) of pages in and out of main memory.
Fundamental process states are new/ready/running/blocked(waiting)/terminated; 'Blocked' IS fundamental, while 'Executed' is not a state (the state is 'running/executing'), so the odd one out is B.
LRU = Least Recently Used.
802.11 WLAN family is branded Wi-Fi; Ethernet is 802.3 and WiMAX is 802.16.

Standard textbook justification: the kernel is memory-resident because it is the most frequently used/called portion of the OS; A (interrupt handling) is a consequence, not the reason. Only two options given and no source key, so low confidence.

blindAnswer: == compares references (same memory location); .equals() compares object values/content (for primitives == compares values)
reason: Java semantics: == is reference (identity) comparison, .equals() is value/content comparison as overridden by the class.
sourceAnswer: == is a reference comparison (both point at the same memory location) where. equals () evaluate the comparison of values of objects

reason: NAND output is 0 only when both inputs are 1.
Algorithm efficiency is measured by time complexity and space complexity.
cin is an object of class istream, not a class; therefore 'it is a class of which stream is an object' is the false statement.
Constructors can be overloaded arbitrarily; a class may have any number of them.
A schema is the overall logical description of the database: data elements, records/files and the relationships between records.
Boehm's spiral model is the risk-driven process model.
DDCMP is byte-count/character oriented so framing needs no special hardware, whereas bit-oriented SDLC needs hardware flag detection and bit de-stuffing to find message start.

blindAnswer: Mealy output depends on present state AND present inputs; Moore output depends on present state only
reason: Standard FSM definition of Mealy vs Moore output functions.
sourceAnswer:
- Mealy FSM depends on present state and present input
- Moore FSM depends on present state only

reason: Figure table: 00 -> Q (hold), 01 -> 0 (reset), 10 -> 1 (set), 11 -> Q' (toggle); the toggle on 11 uniquely identifies JK (SR would be invalid there).
Search/insert/delete in a balanced BST run in O(log n) (height-proportional); worst case degenerates to O(n).
